title: Function Keys, Shortcuts, and Wildcards
text: Function Keys, Shortcuts, and Wildcards You can access menus, retrieve data, and enter and manipulate data using keyboard shortcuts and mouse actions. Functions keys and shortcuts are defined in the keyboard translation table and can be overridden by the definitions in your system's initialization file. Because the keys used to access specific functions can be changed, they are often referred to by logical names in the documentation. Gold Key The Gold key is the logical name of a special Uniface character used for entering structure editor functions, profile characters and subfield separator characters from the keyboard. On Microsoft Windows, the default Gold key is the + key on the numeric keypad. An alternative mapping is Ctrl + Shift + F1 , which is useful on laptops without a numeric keyboard, or on Mac keyboards running Windows. The Gold key is typically used in sequence with other keys. For example, you can use Gold followed by a semi-colon ( ; ) to enter a subfield character used in Uniface lists. In the Uniface IDE, profile characters and subfield separators are shown in inverse. In documentation, they are represented with an underline. For example, in a Uniface list, the subfield characters are represented as ; : apples ; oranges ; pears Copy Wildcards and Search Profile Characters To retrieve or specify only selected data, you can use wildcards and other profile characters . Valid Uniface profile characters are the Gold key followed by one of: *, ?, =, >, <, !, &, or |. Note: Gold profile characters cannot be used in Dynamic or Static Server Page components but you can use the backslash ( \ ) instead of Gold for profile characters. Table 1. Profile Characters Character Meaning Gold * Match 0 through n characters of a string, whether they are printable or not. Cannot be used to match numeric values. Gold ? Match any single character in a string, whether it is printable or not. Cannot be used for to match numeric values. Gold = Equals (if used without a qualifier: "is null"). Gold < Less than Gold > Greater than Gold ! Logical NOT Gold & Logical AND Gold | Gold ; Logical OR Note: When Gold ; is used as a profile character, it is treated as a logical OR, not as a list separator. Table 2. Related learning videos Title link Web Profiling Related Links Gold and Super Key Combinations Gold and Super key combinations are defined with ^GOLD_BASE_SET. By default, these key combinations are available in all keyboard translation tables in the USYS library that support a Gold key. Profile Characters in Web Applications In Web applications, the GOLD character is not recognized, so it is not possible to use the normal Uniface profile characters, such as GOLD * or GOLD ?.
